title: The Fisherman and His Wife
lines: A poor fisherman caught a fish that was actually an enchanted prince.
The fish begged for his life, and the kind fisherman threw him back into the sea.
When the fisherman told his wife about the magical fish, she demanded that he go back and ask for wishes.
The fisherman did not want to, but his wife insisted.
He called to the fish, and the fish appeared.
The fisherman's wife asked first for a cottage, and the fish granted it.
But soon she wanted a castle, and the fish gave her one.
She was never satisfied and asked for more and more power.
She wanted to be queen, then empress, then pope, then even to command the sun and moon.
When she asked to control the sun and moon, the fish's patience ran out.
He took it all away, and she found herself back in the old hovel where she had started.
The lesson was that greed and discontent lead to ruin.
